articles.title: NVIDIA Introduces New Jetson Thor Computers to Advance Mainstream Robotics and Edge AI
articles.summary: General-purpose robots and autonomous machines are moving from research labs to real-world mass-market deployment, creating demand for compact, power-efficient AI supercomputers capable of running foundation models at the edge. articles.title: NVIDIA and Japan Bring Full-Stack AI and Robotics to Every Industry
articles.summary: Home to leading manufacturers, robotics pioneers, infrastructure builders and iconic gaming companies, of course, Japan is one of the world’s centers of AI — building across the full stack with NVIDIA technologies. articles.summary: Enterprises have plenty of powerful models to choose from. The real test is whether the AI an enterprise builds uniquely addresses the needs of the business: improving workflows, tapping into domain knowledge and exceeding standards for accuracy and trust.

articles.title: Why Performance per Watt Is the Ultimate Metric for AI Infrastructure Efficiency
articles.summary: Power is AI infrastructure’s inescapable constraint. How many tokens an AI factory can generate within a fixed power budget determines its revenue and profitability.
